summary: Controls the WSL 2 memory limit through %UserProfile%\.wslconfig. The app now writes the documented [wsl2] memory setting instead of a registry surrogate.

setting.name: WSL 2 Memory Limit
setting.casual_explanation: WSL 2 can use a lot of RAM. Microsoft documents memory limits in .wslconfig, and this tweak writes that documented setting directly.
setting.why_people_change_it: People limit WSL 2 memory when Linux development workloads consume too much RAM and make the Windows side of the machine feel sluggish.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: A memory limit can protect the Windows host from WSL overuse. The app now targets the documented .wslconfig [wsl2] memory surface, so the behavior is explicit and reversible.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents WSL configuration in .wslconfig and describes memory as a WSL 2 host setting under [wsl2]. The current app writes %UserProfile%\.wslconfig with a [wsl2] memory setting, which matches the documented surface.

allowed_values.label: WSL default memory behavior
allowed_values.meaning: WSL 2 uses its normal default memory behavior because no explicit memory limit is configured in .wslconfig.

allowed_values.label: Configured memory limit
allowed_values.meaning: WSL 2 memory is explicitly limited in .wslconfig using a size value such as 4GB or 8GB.

targets.notes: The documented WSL memory control lives in .wslconfig under the [wsl2] section.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes %UserProfile%\.wslconfig with [wsl2] memory=4GB.

windows_defaults.label: WSL default
windows_defaults.applies_to: WSL users who have not explicitly configured a memory cap in .wslconfig

states.rationale: Leave WSL 2 on its default memory behavior until an explicit cap is configured.

windows_defaults.notes: This record now tracks the documented WSL memory setting directly.

recommended_profiles.label: Set WSL 2 memory limit
intended_for: Users who want to cap WSL 2 memory usage
avoid_for: Users who need WSL 2 to dynamically consume available RAM without a cap

states.rationale: Microsoft documents memory under .wslconfig [wsl2]; the app writes the same surface with a 4GB cap.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a fixed WSL 2 memory ceiling.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want WSL 2 to use more than 4GB when available.
tradeoffs: Reversible by removing the setting or changing the value in .wslconfig.
recommended_profiles.notes: apply_allowed=true. The app now matches the documented .wslconfig memory surface.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Advanced settings configuration in WSL
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents WSL 2 memory limits in the .wslconfig file under the [wsl2] section.

decision.why: Microsoft documents WSL 2 memory limits through the .wslconfig file under the [wsl2] section. The app now writes that documented surface directly, so the record is validated and publishable.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: The .wslconfig file configures settings globally for all Linux distributions running with WSL 2.; [wsl2]; memory=4GB
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft documents WSL 2 memory limits in %UserProfile%\.wslconfig under the [wsl2] section using the memory setting. The current app writes the same documented surface.
